title: Does Anno have a successor
wikitext:
[]: Does Anno have a successor?             Quote Post by ChaddyManPrime » Tue May 11, 2021 9:58 pm I was thinking, if this is the end of Anno doing Eva related things, who's gonna take care of it after him? Has he handed the reigns over to someone already?

[]: Re: Does Anno have a successor?             Quote Post by FelipeFritschF » Wed May 12, 2021 12:08 am I'd assume Nishigori or Tsurumaki are the most likely candidates amongst his protegés, but it's more likely he'll hand it off to people uninvolved with Eva. Some people have been floating around an Eva by Makoto Shinki, for instance. The full quote from the 2016 interview is: For the future, he wishes Evangelion will become a cultural icon, one of the cornerstones of anime, much like Gundam, which has become an institution in and of itself. Like Gundam, where many creators can play around with the elements and create their own worlds, it is Anno's hope that Evangelion can also be set free, to be reconstructed and reinterpreted in many ways by many other artists.

[]: Re: Does Anno have a successor?             Quote Post by Kendrix » Wed May 12, 2021 8:17 am i kinda hope it wont happen, these sorts of things are just rarely ever good. It works with other mecha franchises because the focus is the robots or politics/military tropes, but with EVA, the wow factor is just too tied up in this particular set of characters

[]: Re: Does Anno have a successor?             Quote Post by Konja7 » Wed May 12, 2021 8:25 am Kendrix wrote: i kinda hope it wont happen, these sorts of things are just rarely ever good.

[]: Re: Does Anno have a successor?             Quote Post by Arcadia's legacy » Wed May 12, 2021 9:12 am I agree that the main draw of Eva is its characters, however, if it were up to me, a new series would, while still largely character focused, focus its attention on new characters. We've already seen our pilot's journeys twice now (three times if you count the manga), i personally would not mind if we had an entirely new cast in the limelight

[]: Re: Does Anno have a successor?             Quote Post by Zusuchan » Wed May 12, 2021 9:21 am Anno won't do more Eva himself, but he has stated he'll quality-control future Eva works. As for who'd make the next Eva anime, I don't know.

[]: Re: Does Anno have a successor?             Quote Post by bogusman » Wed May 12, 2021 10:03 am Characters in Eva are the main soul of this franchise. Should it be made with completely new and different characters it would need a lot of time to build fan's interest, and an amazingly good designer and well planned. Sadamoto's design of the original main characters are so amazing, that people so attached & the fanchise has difficulties and almost impossible to stray from it. Such as Asuka in rebuild. They had a plan for mari to take over the roles/position but in the end they still cannot. Or might as well rebrand Dual or Darling in the franxx with Eva label.

[]: Re: Does Anno have a successor?             Quote Post by GhostlyOcam » Wed May 12, 2021 10:27 am FelipeFritschF wrote: I'd assume Nishigori or Tsurumaki are the most likely candidates amongst his protegés, but it's more likely he'll hand it off to people uninvolved with Eva.

[]: Re: Does Anno have a successor?             Quote Post by Konja7 » Wed May 12, 2021 11:06 am GhostlyOcam wrote: I feel like Nishigori is also the franchise's current "secondary face" after Sadamoto, with how he's been providing nearly every official arts (e.g. poster, Shin-related merchs, theatrical phamplet, Newtype cover) aside from being Shin's chief AD. Shin itself has a notable number of younger core staffs and I can see that as Anno injecting "new blood" both into Khara and perhaps Eva itself, I'm sure at least some of them has their own ideas and takes of Eva lore. Believe it or not, judging from the social media reactions, a lot fans seems to clamor for a derived work with Ryoji Kaji Jr. as the protagonist so I think they're ready for a Shinji-less Eva spin-off.

[]: Re: Does Anno have a successor?             Quote Post by EvaChero » Wed May 12, 2021 11:06 am other than depicting what happened during the "time skip" where else could the franchise go? It's really all about Tokyo3 and the central characters with the world on their shoulders...the rest of the planet seemed pretty clue less.

[]: Re: Does Anno have a successor?             Quote Post by Kendrix » Wed May 12, 2021 11:40 am Well, Madoka got a lot of spinoff mangas & games set between the first loop & the series...

[]: Re: Does Anno have a successor?             Quote Post by FelipeFritschF » Wed May 12, 2021 4:09 pm Konja7 wrote: Shinji is the main character, but he wouldn't be the character more difficult to replace, since he isn't so profitable in the merchandise aspect (OTOH, his Eva is the face of the franchise and extremely profitable). It's Asuka, Rei and Kaworu (and even Mari) who are extremely profitable in the merchandise aspect. It would be pretty difficult to replace them.

[]: Re: Does Anno have a successor?             Quote Post by Kendrix » Wed May 12, 2021 4:35 pm FelipeFritschF wrote: He still tops popularity polls himself, in fact he was at #3 I think in Newtype last month.

[]: Re: Does Anno have a successor?             Quote Post by Konja7 » Wed May 12, 2021 4:41 pm FelipeFritschF wrote: He's often used as an accessory to those characters, though. Shinji himself isn't unpopular at all, just not marketable per se. There's a difference. He still tops popularity polls himself, in fact he was at #3 I think in Newtype last month.

[]: Re: Does Anno have a successor?             Quote Post by Pluto » Wed May 12, 2021 5:27 pm Many Japanese youtube speculation videos mention that they would love to see a timeskip side story movie which I think would totally be doable without Anno as Shinji is not directly a part of a story. I myself would love to see the movie which would probably be titled: シンエヴァンゲリオン：空間の14年. I work pretty close to Khara studios so I was thinking of sending them a letter regarding this concept. Another idea would be a reboot like gundam which would be more in line with Anno's stated intentions. Now who will direct it? I have no idea. Probably someone famous

[]: Re: Does Anno have a successor?             Quote Post by GhostlyOcam » Wed May 12, 2021 6:26 pm ↑Hidenori Matsubara actually tweeted something about that and what he said is pretty much "it ain't happening", although they seem aware people will definitely be curious about it. Translation: Matsubara's son: "Dad, when will you tell us about the missing 14 years?" Matsubara's wife: "I like the backstory between Gendo, Yui, and Mari. I want Evangelion: Zero ." Matsubara: "Maybe we'll do that if Khara ran out of money." tweet-body — twitter-user (@username) date document.querySelectorAll('blockquote.twitter-tweet > a').forEach((t) => t.href = t.href.replace('x.com', 'twitter.com'))

[]: Re: Does Anno have a successor?             Quote Post by Konja7 » Wed May 12, 2021 6:37 pm GhostlyOcam wrote: ↑Hidenori Matsubara actually tweeted something about that and what he said is pretty much "it ain't happening", although they seem aware people will definitely be curious about it.

[]: Re: Does Anno have a successor?             Quote Post by Kendrix » Wed May 12, 2021 7:39 pm I honestly don't WANT too much explained or further mucked up tbh. I'd take a planned, natural end any day over something that goes on in a zombie-like manner until nobody is listening anymore.

[]: Re: Does Anno have a successor?             Quote Post by FelipeFritschF » Wed May 12, 2021 11:26 pm Pluto wrote: I work pretty close to Khara studios so I was thinking of sending them a letter regarding this concept. s

[]: Re: Does Anno have a successor?             Quote Post by bogusman » Thu May 13, 2021 12:11 am SPOILER: Show
